notes: Wire hollow-check warning into pb validate (non-fatal, --strict makes it fatal); update cmdBootstrap playbook.yaml template with north_star/fixation/hardening/cycle; update cmdBootstrap SKILL.md template with phase-loop and memory-precedence; add cycle/reflect to CLAUDE.md commands; add test-hollow-validate.mjs
Designed loop epochs: active loop registry, per-loop logs/artifacts, tracked process cleanup, explicit close gate, and failure quarantine before starting a second loop.
Extended loop epoch design with learning reflections: memory/lessons.ndjson, pb learn, promotion into project memory/backlog/skills, and a failed-loop learning gate before starting the next loop.

Implemented v0.3 loop epochs and learning: loop new/status/close/quarantine, learn/status, run/ps/stop tracking, loop_id stamping, checkpoint/anchor/report/status awareness, docs, tests, and version bump.
v0.3 adds explicit loop epochs and learning reflections so failed/contaminated loops can be closed, quarantined, learned from, and followed by a cleaner smarter loop.

Added runCommandSync + spawnCommand helpers next to resolveExecutable in scripts/pb.mjs (after line 96). Both helpers use shell:true on Windows so cmd.exe does PATHEXT lookup and dispatches .cmd/.bat shims (npm, pnpm, yarn). Routed runChecks (line 459) and runCommands (line 482) through runCommandSync. Routed cmdRun (line 1311) through spawnCommand. Added scripts/test-win32-runner.mjs (npm shim + pnpm/yarn skip-if-missing + node regression + pb run -- background path). Wired into package.json test script. pb validate green; full suite 9/9 green.

Added 3 numbered Operating Rules to memory/project-memory.md: (7) execFileSync/spawn with shell:false cannot launch .cmd/.bat shims on Windows; (8) shell:true does NOT re-quote argv — pass bare name + rely on PATHEXT; (9) risk surface is user-defined acceptance_checks and pb run --. Verified by scripts/check-win32-rules.mjs (PASS) and pb validate (green).

Extended scripts/test-win32-runner.mjs with full pb run -- lifecycle: spawn a sleeper.js (no argv-special chars, sidesteps cmd.exe's >/() interpretation), assert pid recorded in memory/processes.ndjson, assert pb ps lists it alive, assert pb stop flips status to stopped via taskkill /F /T. All 5 sub-tests pass. Updated project-memory rule 8 to reflect actual limitation (cmd.exe-special chars in argv require script-file workaround, not bare -e). Updated check-win32-rules.mjs pattern to match new wording. Full suite 10/10 green.

Phase 2 closed. Made the runner portable to Windows: added runCommandSync + spawnCommand helpers that wrap cmd.exe /d /c on Windows so .cmd/.bat shims (npm/pnpm/yarn) work for both acceptance_checks and pb run --. Empirical lessons learned during this loop: (1) Node's spawn+shell:true eats parens/quotes; (2) windowsVerbatimArguments:true + manual cmdline-string produces a doubled /d /s /c when Node joins argv; (3) cmd.exe /s strips leading+trailing quotes (rule 2) which breaks paths with spaces; (4) Node's default Windows auto-quoting only quotes whitespace/double-quote, not cmd.exe-special chars like ( ) < > & | — the playbook's spawn test now uses a sleeper.js file to avoid argv specials. Carry-forward: the existing skill hardens the rule that ALL new test scripts should default to runCommandSync/spawnCommand helpers instead of raw execFileSync/spawn. North Star still satisfied: done means verified exit code (pb validate green, new self-test green, full suite 10/10).

Phase 4: built the OpenCode adapter (adapters/opencode/: opencode.json, plugin, /pb-loop + /pb-status commands, bounded subagent), added --defer-blocked to the auto-driver with a behavioral test, and registered the opencode hardening profile. All 6 oc-* tasks verified by executable checks (adapter checker + defer-blocked fixture test). Cadence (OpenCode session.idle) now maps onto contract (pb checks) without forking either engine. Honors North Star: done = passing checks, carry-on, survives context loss via anchor/checkpoint hooks.

Built skills/nl (intent grammar: NL -> pb commands) + processes/use-playbook + scripts/lib/nl-router.mjs (canonical matcher, pure stdlib, v1.0.0) + scripts/check-nl-skill.mjs (8 structural checks) + scripts/test-nl-routing.mjs (48 canonical phrases routing to correct intent, precedence edges hold: claim/select, verify/verify-task, record-done/record-blocked, cycle-new/loop-new). Carry-on: no npm deps; matcher is one file. Both skills/index.yaml and processes/index.yaml updated. All three acceptance_checks green; pb validate green; prior adapter/defer tests still green.

Phase 5: shipped skills/nl (the natural-language driver). Intent grammar (18 declared intents) + canonical matcher (scripts/lib/nl-router.mjs, pure stdlib v1.0.0) + structural checker (8 checks) + behavioral fixture (48 phrases + precedence edges). Carry-on: no npm deps. Skill doc + process doc keep the agent honest about precedence (claim/select, verify/verify-task, record-done/record-blocked, cycle-new/loop-new) so the routing never silently degrades. North Star satisfied: every claim is verified by an executable check, not by prose.

Added modes/coding.yaml (first mode; directive empty by intent = inherit host prompt), default_mode+modes registry in playbook.yaml, and scripts/check-modes.mjs. Principles tagged kind:check|advice (npm test as the check). Stage-2-ready index pointers reference current global indices.

Added mode resolution (task.mode ?? loop.mode ?? default_mode), pb mode show/set (set scopes to active loop), and additive anchor injection: ONE Mode: line in --brief, principle list + directive in full. Empty directive is non-blocking (prints 'inherits host prompt', exit 0). 8/8 behavioral assertions in test-mode-resolve.mjs.

Added agent_id (PB_AGENT_ID env, --agent override, default 'agent'), claimed_by, and resolved mode to the claim ledger (backlog-state.json — now the single claim authority) and to every journal entry. Claim resolves mode as task.mode??loop.mode??default_mode. Full npm test regression green. test-agent-identity.mjs 7/7.

Wired kind:check principles into validation: pb mode check + pb validate --mode run the active mode's check: commands via the Windows-safe runner (npm shim works). advice principles never gate. Plain pb validate stays structural-only (no recursion: a check principle may call validate but never validate --mode). test-mode-principles.mjs 6/6.

Scoped the one-in-progress guard per agent_id via taskHolder() (claimed_by ?? agent_id ?? default 'agent'): two agents can hold tasks concurrently; same agent still one-at-a-time. Scoped the checkpoint multi-wip warning per holder too. Cycle/phase blockers stay shared by design (all agents work one phase). Full npm test regression green incl. existing single-agent invariant. test-wip-per-agent 5/5.

Added O_EXCL lockfile (backlog-state.json.lock) with stale-lock breaker + sync backoff; wrapped the claim's read-verify-write so the candidate is re-checked as still-todo INSIDE the lock. Two agents racing one task resolve to exactly one winner (loser rejected, no double-claim). Carry-on: a file, no daemon/DB. Race test 8 concurrent racers x3 runs all green; full npm test regression green. test-atomic-claim 4/4.

Mode-routed claiming: next filters candidates by the claiming agent's mode (--mode <id> override, else resolved loop.mode??default). A task tagged mode M is claimable only by an M agent; UNTAGGED tasks claimable by any mode (never starved). Clear 'no tasks match mode' message when only other-mode work remains. Ties tracks A+B. test-mode-routing 5/5; full npm test green.

Phase 6 (multi-agent + modes) complete: 7 tasks, each shipped with a behavioral test, all green; full npm test regression intact throughout. North Star preserved exactly — the enforcement floor never weakened. Track A (modes): coding is the first persona pack; directive empty-by-intent = inherit host prompt (non-blocking, enforced); principles tagged check|advice with kind:check wired into pb mode check / validate --mode (not faith-based); anchor injection stayed tiny (one Mode: line in --brief). Track B (multi-agent): agent_id/claimed_by/mode stamped on claims+journal with backlog-state.json as the single claim authority; one-in-progress scoped per agent_id; O_EXCL lockfile makes claims atomic (8 concurrent racers -> exactly one winner, 3x); mode-routed claiming lets a pool share one board, untagged tasks never starved. The mode field does double duty (persona + work-router) exactly as designed. Carry-on held (no deps; .mjs checkers; Windows runner rules). Carries forward to Stage 2: skills/processes move into packs by repointing modes' skills_index/processes_index inward; index resolution becomes active_mode union engine. Caught a real duplicate-declaration bug via a per-task test before record — the test-per-artifact discipline paid off.

Added union resolvers: resolvedSkillEntries()/resolvedProcessEntries() merge engine globals with the active mode's pack-local indices (engine ids win on collision; packs additive). Routed skillFor, validate's index reads, and cmdList through them. coding's pointers == global files so union == globals (no behavior change; live list shows all engine skills). test-pack-resolution 5/5; full npm test green.

Extended check-modes.mjs: for any mode whose skills_index/processes_index points under modes/ (a pack), assert the index parses, referenced files exist, and the pack dir is carry-on (no package.json/node_modules). Modes on global indices (coding) exempt. test-pack-carryon 4/4 (clean passes; missing-file, package.json, node_modules each fail); check-modes 7/7 on real repo; validate green.

Created the real modes/demo pack (own skill demo-skill + process demo-proc, registered only in pack-local indices) and registered it in the master. test-demo-pack 5/5: demo-skill+demo-proc resolve under demo, engine 'core' still resolves (union), demo-skill hidden under coding, and scripts/pb.mjs contains ZERO 'demo' code — mounts purely from YAML. check-modes 7/7, validate green, full npm test green. Stage 2 existence proof complete.

Phase 7 (Stage 2 — composable pack indices) complete: 3 tasks, each with a behavioral test, all green; full npm test regression intact throughout; North Star + floor untouched. pack-resolution: skill/process lookups resolve as engine-globals UNION active-mode pack-local indices (engine ids win; packs additive); skillFor/validate/cmdList routed through it; coding's pointers == globals so coding behavior is unchanged (live list shows all engine skills). pack-carryon-validate: check-modes now validates pack-local indices (parse + referenced files exist) and enforces carry-on (no package.json/node_modules in a pack dir); global-index modes exempt. demo-pack-proof: a REAL modes/demo pack (own skill+process, registered only in pack-local indices) mounts purely from YAML — test asserts it resolves under demo, is hidden under coding, and that pb.mjs contains ZERO 'demo' code. The phase-6 prediction held exactly: Stage 2 was a repoint + resolver change, not a rewrite, because the skills_index/processes_index pointers were placed in phase 6. The engine is now domain-pure: coding is the first pack, a non-coding pack mounts with no engine change. Next natural step (future): migrate the existing engine skills that are coding-specific into modes/coding/ to shrink the global indices, and add real domain packs.

Added blogwatch mode pack: modes/blogwatch.yaml, pack-local watch-feeds skill/process, registered in playbook.yaml, plus scripts/check-blogwatch-mode.mjs and scripts/test-blogwatch-pack.mjs. All acceptance checks green.
Built closed-loop daily monitor: scripts/pb-daily-monitor.mjs, daily-monitor skill/process, config, ORCHESTRATOR.md, auto logs, plus check/test scripts. All green.
